title: orchis maculata subsp. elodes
intro: Orchis maculata subsp. elodes, also known as the autumn lady's tresses, is a type of orchid plant that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The autumn lady's tresses is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and alleviating menstrual cramps, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
